description_long: This is a vintage-style portable radio receiver with a rectangular, boxy form and a prominent telescopic metal antenna extending from the top. The front face features a dense array of buttons, dials, and switches, as well as a large speaker grille and a frequency display window. The casing appears to be made primarily of black and metallic materials, with some turquoise or teal highlights on the controls. The sides of the radio show additional controls and possibly a carrying handle, while the back is mostly solid with a textured pattern and a small window or label area. The overall design suggests durability and functionality, suitable for both indoor and outdoor urban environments. The object is compact enough to be carried by hand and placed on various surfaces.
description: A compact, vintage-style portable radio receiver with a telescopic antenna, numerous buttons and dials, and a prominent speaker grille. The casing is mostly black with metallic and turquoise accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the slim profile, antenna, and a vertical row of controls or ports.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the main control panel, speaker grille, frequency display, and brand label.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a large knob and additional controls, as well as the antenna base.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a textured back panel with a small window or label area and no major controls.
